# Shared Claude Code Skills

> Source: https://github.com/isnbh0/shared

A collection of reusable Claude Code skills and command aliases, distributed as Claude Code plugins. This file instructs LLMs on how to install skills from this repository into a user's project.

## Installation Instructions

### Option A: Plugin (recommended)

First, register the marketplace:

```
/plugin marketplace add isnbh0/shared
```

Then install individual skills:

```
/plugin install interview@isnbh0
/plugin install spex@isnbh0
/plugin install critique@isnbh0
/plugin install macros@isnbh0
/plugin install study@isnbh0
```

> **Note:** Only the five skills above are published to the marketplace. The `doubt`, `consensus`, `seq`, `rigor`, `askme`, `delegate`, and `tmi` skills are included in the `macros` plugin. The remaining skills (report-writer, rigorous-debug, skill-writer, phaser) must be installed via copy or symlink (Options B/C below).

### Option B: Install individual skills (copy)

Copy only the skills the user needs. Each skill is a self-contained directory under `plugins/<name>/skills/<name>/`.

```bash
# Clone the repository (if not already cloned)
git clone git@github.com:isnbh0/shared.git ~/shared

# Copy a specific skill
cp -r ~/shared/plugins/<skill-name>/skills/<skill-name> /path/to/project/.claude/skills/
```

### Option C: Install individual skills (symlink)

Symlink individual skills for automatic updates when the source repo is pulled.

```bash
ln -s ~/shared/plugins/<skill-name>/skills/<skill-name> /path/to/project/.claude/skills/<skill-name>
```


## Available Skills

### Published (marketplace)

### interview
Structured requirements discovery through conversational interviews. Extracts requirements, constraints, and design decisions through guided Q&A. Produces timestamped synthesis documents.

Invocation: `/interview <topic> [--ref <path>] [--workspace <dir>]`

### spex
Two-phase specification and implementation workflow that separates planning from execution. Write specs, get them reviewed, then implement against them.

Skills: `/spex:write`, `/spex:write-phased`, `/spex:implement`

### critique
External AI critique via CLI tools (Codex, Gemini). Gets an independent second opinion on specs, code, or recent changes using sandboxed execution.

Invocation: `/critique:codex [file-path] [focus]`, `/critique:gemini [file-path] [focus]`

### macros
Subagent orchestration workflows and session modes. Includes map-reduce for parallel task splitting, doubt for research-backed code critique, consensus for multi-agent review, seq for serial blind passes, and rigor for session-level correctness enforcement.

Invocations:
- `/macros:mapreduce <task> [--workspace <dir>]` — split tasks into chunks, dispatch parallel subagents, consolidate results
- `/macros:doubt ["freeform question"]` — spawn a blind subagent that reads code, verifies assumptions against web sources, applies fixes, and reports concerns ranked by severity
- `/macros:consensus <count>` — run N blind agents on the same job in parallel, merge findings into consensus/unique/conflicts (no edits for concurrent safety)
- `/macros:seq <count>` — run N serial blind passes with commits between rounds; requires clean worktree
- `/macros:rigor` — activate Rigor Mode for the session: prioritize correctness, thorough investigation, and web-grounded verification over minimalism
- `/macros:askme` — shorthand: stop and ask the user to clarify ambiguities or make decisions instead of assuming
- `/macros:delegate` — shorthand: prefer subagents to save context space and parallelize independent subtasks
- `/macros:tmi` — flag content that only makes sense if you were in the room when it was written; report by default, edit if explicitly instructed

### study
Document-grounded Socratic study sessions on any URL or local file. Calibrates to user familiarity, persists session notes with resumption across sittings, and supports freeform persona/behavior customization via config.

Invocation: `/study <uri>`

### Other (copy / symlink only)

### report-writer
Structured technical analysis and debugging reports with standardized sections (Executive Summary, Key Findings, Root Cause Analysis, Recommendations).

Invocation: `/report-writer [topic] [--workspace <dir>]`

### rigorous-debug
Evidence-based debugging protocol using the scientific method. Enforces hypothesis-driven investigation with controlled experiments. Requires one-time project-specific initialization before first use.

Invocation: `/rigorous-debug`

### skill-writer
Tools and guidance for creating effective Claude Code skills following best practices.

Invocation: `/skill-writer`

### phaser
Battle-tested patterns and best practices for Phaser 3 game development. Passive knowledgebase — automatically consulted when writing Phaser code. Covers scene architecture, physics, animations, input, object pooling, performance, and more.

## Skill Dependencies

Each skill is fully self-contained. There are no cross-skill dependencies. Install only what you need.

The skills (`write`, `write-phased`, `implement`) are included in the `spex` plugin.

## File Structure Reference

Skills live under `plugins/` as independent plugin packages:

```
shared/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── marketplace.json              # Marketplace catalog
├── plugins/
│   ├── interview/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/interview/
│   ├── spex/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/
│   │       ├── spex/
│   │       ├── write/
│   │       ├── write-phased/
│   │       └── implement/
│   ├── report-writer/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/report-writer/
│   ├── rigorous-debug/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/rigorous-debug/
│   ├── skill-writer/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/skill-writer/
│   ├── critique/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/critique/
│   ├── macros/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/
│   │       ├── macros/
│   │       ├── mapreduce/
│   │       ├── doubt/
│   │       ├── consensus/
│   │       ├── seq/
│   │       ├── rigor/
│   │       ├── askme/
│   │       ├── delegate/
│   │       └── tmi/
│   ├── phaser/
│   │   ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│   │   └── skills/phaser/
│   └── study/
│       ├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json
│       └── skills/study/
└── .claude/
    └── skills/       # Symlinks to plugins/*/skills/* (backward compat)
```

## Workspace Configuration

Skills that produce files (interview, spex, report-writer, macros, study) write to configurable workspace directories. There are no built-in defaults — each skill prompts for setup on first use. Typical values:

| Skill | Default workspace |
|-------|-------------------|
| interview | `.agent-workspace/interviews` |
| spex | `.agent-workspace/specs` |
| report-writer | `.agent-workspace/reports` |
| macros | `.agent-workspace/macros` |
| study | `.agent-workspace/study/sessions` |

Override precedence (first match wins):

1. CLI flag: `--workspace <dir>` — one-off override
2. Local config: `.claude/skill-configs/<skill>/config.local.yaml` — gitignored, personal overrides
3. Project config: `.claude/skill-configs/<skill>/config.yaml` — committed to repo, shared with team

## Notes for LLMs

- When a user asks to "install" or "add" a skill, prefer Option A (plugin) for marketplace skills (interview, spex, critique, macros, study). The `doubt`, `consensus`, `seq`, `rigor`, `askme`, `delegate`, and `tmi` skills are included in the `macros` plugin — no separate install needed. The remaining skills (report-writer, rigorous-debug, skill-writer, phaser) are only available via copy/symlink.
- If the target project already has a `.claude` directory with existing content, never overwrite it. Merge by copying individual skills into the existing `.claude/skills/` directory.
- The `phaser` skill is large (18 knowledgebase files). Only install it if the user is working on a Phaser 3 project.
- The `rigorous-debug` skill requires a one-time initialization step after installation. Inform the user that they will need to run `/rigorous-debug` and complete the setup before it is usable.
- Always verify that the clone location (`~/shared` or user-specified) does not conflict with existing directories before cloning.
